entries.title: The Big Three Reframe What U.A. Student Mastery Can Look Like

context.label: The Big Three are introduced to Class 1-A

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: The Big Three represent U.A. student mastery beyond Class 1-A’s current level and bridge the students toward pro work-study stakes.
content.injection: Use the Big Three as evidence of advanced U.A. competence: still students, but much closer to pro-level field capability.

entries.title: Mirio’s Permeation Skill Comes From Extreme Training

context.label: Mirio demonstrates overwhelming practical skill against Class 1-A

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Mirio’s dominance comes from extreme training, prediction, timing, and control over Permeation’s severe drawbacks.
content.injection: Write Permeation as skill-intensive. Mirio wins through timing, anticipation, and experience, not effortless ghost-mode intangibility.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia
sourceInfo.notes: Draft Saga Lorecard; requires human canon review before reference release.

ability.name: Permeation
constraints: Requires precise body control and timing.
antiLore: Do not make it effortless intangibility.

entries.title: Work Studies Put Students Closer To Real Hero Consequences

context.label: Work studies reopen under stricter post-Kamino conditions

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Work studies expose students to real agency work under pro supervision, creating higher stakes than ordinary internships or school exercises.
content.injection: Treat work-study students as supervised participants in real hero operations. Consequences, secrecy, and legal boundaries matter.

content.fact: Nighteye evaluates Izuku partly through his unresolved belief that Mirio would have been a better successor to All Might.
content.injection: Make Nighteye’s skepticism personal and legacy-driven. He is not simply a stern employer.

entries.title: Nighteye Foresight Is Powerful But Psychologically Burdensome

context.label: Izuku seeks placement at Sir Nighteye Agency

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Nighteye’s Foresight lets him perceive a target’s future after activation, but his faith in its inevitability burdens his choices.
content.injection: Frame Foresight as a powerful prediction tool that shapes Nighteye’s fatalism, not casual omniscience.

entries.title: Overhaul Sees Quirks As Disease And Control Problem

context.label: Overhaul contacts the League of Villains

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Overhaul treats Quirks as disease-like contamination and seeks control, yakuza restoration, and power through Quirk-destroying weapons.
content.injection: Write Overhaul as sterile, controlling, and ideological about Quirks. His cruelty toward Eri is instrumental and obsessive, not random sadism.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia
sourceInfo.notes: Draft Saga Lorecard; requires human canon review before reference release.

ability.name: Overhaul
constraints: Requires touch and can dismantle or reassemble bodies and matter.
antiLore: Do not portray him as a conventional street thug.

entries.title: Overhaul’s Meeting With The League Is A Power Collision

context.label: Overhaul contacts the League of Villains

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Overhaul approaches the League during its post-Kamino instability, creating a power collision between Shigaraki’s emerging leadership and Hassaikai ambitions.
content.injection: Keep the alliance tense and transactional. Shigaraki is not Overhaul’s subordinate, even while the League lacks resources.

entries.title: Magne’s Death Makes The League Grievance Personal

context.label: Overhaul kills Magne and injures Mr. Compress

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Overhaul kills Magne and injures Mr. Compress, turning the League’s later cooperation into a revenge-laden compromise.
content.injection: After the Hassaikai meeting, treat the League as personally wronged by Overhaul. Cooperation should carry resentment and future retaliation pressure.

entries.title: Eri Is Terrified Into Returning To Overhaul

context.label: Eri returns to Overhaul under fear and coercion

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Eri returns to Overhaul because fear and coercion control her, not because she trusts him or chooses him freely.
content.injection: Make Eri’s return a trauma response under Overhaul’s control. Izuku and Mirio’s hesitation should feel morally painful and operationally constrained.

entries.title: Eri’s Rewind Is The Source Of Quirk-Destroying Bullets

context.label: Eri is tied to Quirk-destroying bullets

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Overhaul uses Eri’s Rewind-derived biology to develop Quirk-destroying bullets.
content.injection: Before the investigation confirms it, keep the bullet source hidden. Once known, treat Eri’s rescue as urgent because her body is being exploited to weaponize Quirk erasure.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia

ability.name: Rewind
constraints: Eri does not control Rewind safely.
antiLore: Do not portray Eri as deliberately manufacturing bullets.

entries.title: Raid Planning Requires Police And Multi-Agency Coordination

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: The Hassaikai raid is a coordinated police/pro-hero operation built around investigation, warrants, and urgent rescue concerns.
content.injection: Include police authority, agency coordination, and evidence limits. This is not a student-led assault.

entries.title: Mirio Initially Letting Eri Go Haunts The Rescue Mission

context.label: Eri returns to Overhaul under fear and coercion

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Mirio and Izuku are haunted by returning Eri to Overhaul because legal caution failed a child in immediate danger.
content.injection: Let regret sharpen the rescue urgency; the heroes are trying to correct a failure, not starting from emotional neutrality.

content.fact: The Shie Hassaikai are an organized-crime group seeking relevance in a hero society that has pushed yakuza power downward.
content.injection: Use Hassaikai scenes as organized-crime operations with hierarchy, territory, and restoration goals, not random villain mobs.

entries.title: Rock Lock Distrusts Student Participation In The Raid

content.fact: Rock Lock is skeptical of involving students in a dangerous yakuza raid, reflecting reasonable adult concern rather than hostility.
content.injection: Frame Rock Lock’s objections as professional risk assessment; he is not wrong that students are near lethal danger.

entries.title: Overhaul Values Eri As Resource Not Daughter

context.label: Eri is tied to Quirk-destroying bullets

content.fact: Overhaul treats Eri as a resource and research subject while exploiting a family-like position of control.
